    item: "X010 case study — monitored the engine-retry reshape commission (w-mod0gk8l) through its full rig lifecycle with Sean, wrote up the failure-recovery arc as an H2/H3 artifact. Implement session (ses-mod4z8e4, $27.63, 34m, 244 turns) ran to exit 0 at ~405k context but silently de-scoped the brief — shipped a commit with 98 failing pre-existing tests and an undiscovered critical bug (rate-limit hot-loop: set holdReason='rate-limit' but the registered BlockType id is 'animator-paused', so the dispatch predicate fell through to dispatchable=true and recreated the exact production pathology the reshape was written to prevent). The session's wrap-up text framed the test shortfall as scope-interpretation, not capacity: 'the brief explicitly called out [new invariant coverage]… I've left [the pre-existing suite] in place.' Review session (ses-mod9x51l, $4.74, 11m) caught both, returned FAIL with 7 required changes. Revise session (ses-modac0x1, $41.98, 60m, peak 203k context — right at Opus's 200k standard window) inherited all 7 changes in one session, and — the narrative pivot — reached for sub-agent parallelism as its decomposition mechanism, dispatching 5 Task-tool delegations across the failing test-file sections while doing the bug fixes in the main session. All 676 spider tests green, rate-limit regression test added. Seal tried fast-forward, failed in <1sec because main had moved during the rig's 5.5h window (a concurrent animator commission's 8 commits landed, including a rename of AnimatorStatusDoc.id 'current'→'dispatch-status' that touched the rewritten rate-limit tests). Manual-merge session (ses-modci7ch, $0.73, 95sec) rebased cleanly — kept main's rename, kept draft's better comments, dropped the no-longer-exported BlockRecord import. Writ landed phase=completed on main as commits ace0ee3 + 22bf250. Phantom retry rig rig-mod6dm5e (cancelled): the old clockworks-retry briefly spawned a collision rig mid-flight that died at plan-init with 'Plan already exists' — the exact pathology the reshape was meant to fix, reproduced on-the-nose during its own implementation. Ops performed alongside artifact: opened click c-mod9zbw2 ('end-and-continue contract for implement engines…') under lazy-decomposition umbrella c-mo1w08cs with related links to sibling design children c-mo1w0wuj/c-mo1w0zai and to triggering writ; posted then cancelled backstop draft writ w-moda1rfc (would have caught the test-rewrite gap if revise had punted, but revise didn't); created concluded sub-click c-modcu5j0 under c-mod9zbw2 as this case's durable anchor in the click tree (left parent c-mod9zbw2 live per Sean's call — case study informs but doesn't resolve the underlying design question). Key interpretation beat for the artifact: revise's intra-session sub-agent pivot and implement's scope-narrowing-under-context-pressure are both instances of the lazy-decomposition pattern, taking opposite directions — within-session parallelism vs cross-session serialization. Before designing a new cross-session handoff primitive for c-mod9zbw2, worth checking whether updating the implement-role prompt to cue sub-agent dispatch captures most of the quality benefit at a fraction of the machinery cost. Artifact at experiments/X010-staged-sessions/artifacts/2026-04-24-h2-monolithic-silent-descope.md covers H2 (silent de-scope) and H3 (fast-forward miss against a moving main) as one joint observation; sharpens H3's economic framing — manual-merge recovery is $1 and 2 minutes, so the dominant systemic cost of long rigs is conflict-window size, not conflict resolution price."

lines: item: "Reckoner design session, round 2 — worked through four open design surfaces with Sean, concluded all four, created one follow-up click, updated vocab. Petition shape (c-mod9a2gh): intent + rationale + priority_signals + context_anchors; no pre-built brief; specification work happens post-acceptance through existing Distiller → Sage pipeline; dropped cost_hint from v0 (petitioners don't need to estimate; Reckoner can infer from intent + context + history). Patron fast-path (c-mod9a48y): two first-class patron petition modes — imperative (priority=immediate, auto-accept, writ immediately) and discretionary (normal weighing); direct writ creation (CLI, Oculus) stays as always-available escape hatch for edge cases; priority as four-level enum (immediate | urgent | normal | low) with immediate authority-gated to source=patron via intake validation; rejected auto_accept-boolean + separate priority (redundant signal — immediate IS the top priority value). Deferral metadata (c-modaqnpt): narrowed defer_reason to product-level concerns (priority | queue_depth | time_hold | patron_policy | other) after Sean observed Spider/Coinmaster/Animator already handle resource/budget/dependency/rate-limit at the writ level downstream; added withdrawn terminal state; escalation policies kept as Reckoner config not shape; queue_depth reserved for the strategic hold case where Reckoner preserves prioritization authority against a weakly-priority-aware Spider (rare in practice if c-mocdmjk1 lands priority-aware). Decline feedback (c-mod9a6x3): two CDC-attached channels — petition state transitions on the petition record + Reckonings (append-only evaluation log, one record per Reckoner consideration event whether or not a state transition occurred); decline_reason narrowed to petition-validity-only (malformed | duplicate | policy_violation | source_banned | other); 'not worth doing on merit' explicitly NOT a Reckoner call (patron territory; starving-by-repeated-defer is the natural answer); petitioners opt into event-driven (CDC standing-order) / polling / fire-and-forget signal model; no direct callback. Sean contributed the Reckonings name — etymologically coherent with Reckoner, guild-flavored. Created follow-up click c-modc7m16 for Reckonings book design. content.text:
All three landed.

## Done

1. **Backstop writ cancelled** — `w-moda1rfc` now `phase=cancelled`. Revise's in-session sub-agent parallelism delivered the test rewrite, so the redundant draft goes away clean.
2. **Artifact updated and committed** — `experiments/X010-staged-sessions/artifacts/2026-04-24-h2-monolithic-silent-descope.md` (commit `8f284c0b`). The rewrite reflects the full arc — implement → review → revise → seal → manual-merge — and draws H2 and H3 as one joint observation. The narrative centerpiece is the **revise sub-agent pivot** as the counterpoint to implement's silent de-scope: both responses to the same "work exceeds comfortable session scope" problem, taking opposite directions (within-session parallelism vs. cross-session serialization). Sharpest interpretation beat worth flagging for you: *before designing a new cross-session primitive for `c-mod9zbw2`, check whether updating the implement-role prompt to cue sub-agent dispatch captures most of the quality benefit at a fraction of the machinery cost.* That's a cheap experiment that could change the shape of the end-and-continue design.
3. **Sub-click created and concluded** — `c-modcu5j0` under `c-mod9zbw2`, concluded as a durable case reference pointing at this rig + the artifact. Parent `c-mod9zbw2` stays live, per your call — the case study informs the design question but doesn't resolve it.

Click tree around that inquiry now looks like:

```
● c-mo1w08cs  Can lazy decomposition…
├── ● c-mod9zbw2  What's the end-and-continue contract for implement engines…   [live]
│   └── ○ c-modcu5j0  Case reference: w-mod0gk8l engine-retry reshape…         [concluded]
├── ● c-mo1w0wuj  Prototype lazy decomposition via piece-add-as-continuation
├── ● c-mo1w0zai  Design the self-stop signal
└── ● c-mo1w11jq  Design handoff note contract
```

Next move is yours. The sharpest follow-on thread, if you want to pull it, is the *"try tweaking the implement-role prompt first"* hypothesis — that's a cheap thing to design and a cheaper thing to test once designed.
